visible_observations: A crouched batlike humanoid with enormous upright ears, clawed feet, a fanged face, multiple long limbs, and an asymmetrical black-and-pale fur or skin split draws an organic crescent bow.
The bow is larger than the body and constructed from spined reddish bone and perforated membrane; several taut cords converge across the face, hand, chest, and extended arm.
The low stance, target-facing eyes, drawn cords, and loaded bow make the combat action immediately visible.
defects: Oversized ears, claws, fangs, black fur, and torn membrane combine into a familiar bat-demon vocabulary despite the unusual bow construction.
The bow frame, membrane holes, cords, limbs, fur, and crouched legs overlap densely through the center, making the exact hand and arm count difficult to parse.
Multiple cords cross directly over the face and mouth, obscuring whether the mouth is biting, drawing, restrained, or merely behind the weapon.
The very dark torso and rear body merge into the background while the large bow carries most of the visible design identity.
uncertainties: The image does not clearly establish whether the bow is a separate weapon, transformed anatomy, or a web grown from the body.
The pale and black body halves could indicate species patterning, injury, transformation, or lighting; the cause is unresolved.
The number and attachment points of the upper limbs remain ambiguous because of overlap and foreshortening.

visible_observations: Three black candidates show a hunched side-view archer inside a tall crescent bow, a frontal four-armed robed figure without a visible bow, and a long-legged side-view archer extending a huge membrane bow.
The candidates vary locomotion, arm count, posture, and weapon integration while retaining pointed ears, claws, and a narrow elongated humanoid frame.
defects: The two archer candidates are dominated by nearly the same oversized crescent bow, while the center candidate changes into a generic four-armed monster with no equivalent weapon read.
The thin bowstrings are much less robust than the body and frame contours and would disappear before the weapon action is fully understood.
In the center candidate, four arms, long dress-like lower mass, and hanging strips merge into a vertically symmetrical shape with little directional threat.
uncertainties: The image provides no A, B, C labels or explicit selected-candidate marker.
It is unclear whether the center figure's four arms and the archers' apparent two-arm structures represent the same intended anatomy.

visible_observations: The face points toward the viewer with narrowed fixed eyes, a wrinkled nasal opening, exposed small fangs, and a tight tooth-bearing mouth beneath enormous ears.
Pale fur and skin cover one side while black fur and skin cover the other; taut reddish cords cross diagonally over the forehead, mouth, cheek, and shoulder.
defects: The narrowed eyes and exposed teeth resolve primarily as a generic predatory snarl rather than a more differentiated emotional state.
Cord crossings obscure the mouth corners and divide the face into small regions, reducing expression clarity.
The face provides target fixation but little visible evidence of fear, restraint, conflict, or a want beyond attacking.
uncertainties: Unaided viewing cannot determine whether the mouth is pulling a cord, biting it, trapped by it, or simply seen behind it.
The black-and-pale facial split may be anatomy, corruption, damage, or lighting, and the face shows no unambiguous reaction to it.

visible_observations: At native size, two tall ears, a pale crouched leg, the large curved bow frame, several membrane openings, and a compact dark body remain visible.
The bow crescent and ear pair provide the strongest surviving outline.
defects: The face, teeth, eye direction, cords, limb count, skin split, claws, and most membrane structure collapse at this size.
The torso and folded limbs merge into a dark central knot against the background.
The surviving read is more clearly batlike archer equipment than an individually readable face or body plan.
uncertainties: No neighboring unit icons, gameplay background, selection frame, or motion state are shown, so in-context recognition and weapon readability cannot be determined.
